_meta.note: PARSER-GENERATED. Validate by diff against hand-derived records.

records.name: Square Enix

reasons.notes: Convergence-test candidate — human review needed before consolidation.

records.rawExplanation: *2021–2022 (divest + pivot):* legacy Final Fantasy / Dragon Quest stayed profitable, but the Western studios bled — Marvel's Avengers was a costly flop. In May 2022 Square Enix sold the studios behind Tomb Raider, Deus Ex and Thief — Crystal Dynamics and Eidos-Montréal — to Embracer for $300 million, affecting roughly 1,100 employees, with proceeds earmarked for blockchain, AI and cloud. The price was strikingly small for franchises of that stature, and the blockchain pivot landed poorly. Core business roughly holding; stock derated. 業績 okay, 株価 down → Q2.

records.name: Square Enix

reasons.notes: Convergence-test candidate — human review needed before consolidation.

records.rawExplanation: *2023 (flagship miss):* Final Fantasy XVI launched June 2023 and its PS5 sales reportedly failed to meet the high end of expectations, the share price falling to its lowest since May 2022 — nearly $2bn of market value lost since the game's release, compounded by the Forspoken flop. Business disappointing, stock down → Q4.

records.name: Square Enix

reasons.notes: Convergence-test candidate — human review needed before consolidation.
Convergence-test candidate — human review needed before consolidation.

records.rawExplanation: *2024 (the stumble + reset):* on April 30, 2024 the company disclosed about ¥22.1 billion (~$140M) in content-abandonment losses for the fiscal year ended March 2024, cancelling multiple in-development titles to be "more selective and focused". In May 2024, new CEO Takashi Kiryu unveiled a restructuring and a multiplatform strategy alongside layoffs at US and EU offices, conceding the title portfolio hadn't been managed well, with FF7 Rebirth and FF16 profits not meeting expectations. Losses + weak HD games, stock depressed → Q4.

records.name: Square Enix

reasons.notes: Convergence-test candidate — human review needed before consolidation.

records.rawExplanation: *2025 (sales down, profits up):* the restructuring began paying off in an unusual shape — net game sales fell sharply (an ¥11bn drop in the April–June 2025 quarter) because no major titles shipped, yet operating income rose on cost control. Revenue ✕ but profitability ◎ — a genuine split-signal transition year; stock recovering. → Q1/Q2, low confidence.

records.name: Square Enix

reasons.notes: Convergence-test candidate — human review needed before consolidation.

records.rawExplanation: *2026 (restructuring pays off):* for the fiscal year ended March 31, 2026, net sales were ¥297.6 billion, down 8.3%, while operating income rose 34.9% to ¥54.7 billion, operating margin improved to 18.4% from 12.5%, and net profit rose 21.3% to ¥29.6 billion despite ¥12.1 billion in reorganization costs, helped by FX gains; full-year profit forecasts were raised during the year as nine-month results beat expectations on improved game profitability and stronger royalty income, with sales guidance unchanged. Again revenue ✕ / profit ◎◎, stock recovering on the turnaround → Q1, with the revenue-down caveat. Bears: top-line shrinking, heavy reliance on cost-cuts and a thin upcoming release slate.
